fields.description:
## Context / Problem

Ticket 4af8 injected the vendor mapper entrypoints, but four core modules still reach the
Jira adapter for *scalar* concerns: `applier.py:603-605` (imports `adapters.jira.acli_subprocess`
and calls `resolve_jira_settings(project_default="DIG").project` for the cross-project safety
guard), `fetcher.py:379-381` and `fetcher.py:441-443` (`resolve_jira_settings().project`,
un-defaulted, for JQL scoping and parent enrichment), `_attestation.py:65-79`
(`resolve_jira_settings(project_default="DIG")` + the JIRA_URL/JIRA_USER/JIRA_API_TOKEN
fail-fast), and `apply_handlers.py:39` (`from …adapters.jira.acli_subprocess import
AssigneeNotFoundError`). Epic bbf1 AC3 requires zero `adapters.jira` imports in core.

An earlier version of this ticket also moved the vendor-shaped field-diff helpers
(`diff_fields`/`extract_field`/`assignee_matches`/`local_type_to_remote`) onto the Backend
port; plan review blocked that (correctly) because those helpers are parameterized on Jira
snapshot dicts, violating ADR 0035 §(d) ("core owns diff/apply; the port only translates").
That concern is now owned by the sibling canonical-shape differ stories under epic bbf1 —
this ticket is re-scoped to the scalar, translation-only port surface that the block never
applied to.

## Approach

Extend the `Backend` Protocol in `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/_backend.py` with
scalar, read-only members — none parameterized on vendor shapes:

- `project` — the project/scope key with the applier's default semantics (Jira:
  `resolve_jira_settings(project_default="DIG").project`), consumed by the `applier.py:601`
  cross-project guard.
- `query_project` — the un-defaulted variant (Jira: `resolve_jira_settings().project`),
  preserving the fetcher's fail-closed behavior when unset, consumed at `fetcher.py:379-381`
  and `fetcher.py:441-443`.
- `assert_env_ready()` — raises a new neutral `BackendEnvError(RuntimeError)` (also defined in
  `_backend.py`) listing the missing connection essentials; JiraBackend carries the current
  `_attestation.py:72-79` JIRA_URL/JIRA_USER/JIRA_API_TOKEN check.
- A neutral `BackendAssigneeNotFoundError` exception defined in `_backend.py`;
  `adapters/jira/acli_subprocess.py`'s `AssigneeNotFoundError` subclasses it, and
  `apply_handlers.py` catches the neutral base (soft-fail semantics unchanged).

All three accessors sit on the `Backend` facade Protocol itself (not a new capability
sub-protocol): every backend has *some* scoping/readiness story, and a project-less backend
(e.g. a flat-list GitHub backend) returns its configured scope key (repo slug) or raises
`BackendEnvError` from `assert_env_ready()` — the fail-closed semantics the consumers already
have. Update the S2 contract suite and the test-only `FakeBackend` in the same change so
every in-tree Backend implementor satisfies the widened Protocol (structural typing breaks
loudly at typecheck otherwise — that IS the expand step; there are no out-of-tree
implementors yet, per ADR 0035).

Route the four modules through the backend instance already in scope where one exists,
otherwise through the registry seam (`select_backend(load_config())` — the exact pattern
already proven at `outbound_differ.py:450-454`), and delete their
`adapters.jira`/`acli_subprocess` imports. Extend
`tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/test_backend_neutrality.py` with a NEW full-tree AST walker:
the existing `_module_level_imported_names` helper walks only `tree.body` (top-level nodes),
but all four vendor-import sites being removed are function-nested lazy imports
(`fetcher.py:379`, `fetcher.py:441`, `applier.py:603`, `_attestation.py:65`) — so the new
case uses `ast.walk` over the whole tree, collecting every `ast.Import`/`ast.ImportFrom` at
any nesting depth, and asserts none resolves under `adapters.jira`/`acli_subprocess` for the
four modules. (`inbound_probe.py:65-67` uses the same `resolve_jira_settings()` pattern but
is deliberately NOT in this ticket's scope: it is owned whole — REST mechanics and all — by
sibling story `aff0-b0d9-e11c-4b6a` under the same epic, which relocates it behind a
`SupportsAbsenceProbe` capability; splitting the file between two tickets would create an
edit-set overlap.)

ADR 0035 §(d)'s pinned-interface table gains a row documenting the new scalar members and
`BackendEnvError`/`BackendAssigneeNotFoundError`, so future backend implementors have a
canonical reference for what the port now mandates.

Rejected alternative: extending the port with the vendor-shaped diff helpers — blocked by
plan review as an ADR 0035 §(d) violation (a GitHub backend would have to implement
`extract_field(jira_fields, …)`); the differ is made neutral by the canonical-shape sibling
stories instead. Salvage note: Gerrit change 920 (worktree `impl-4af8-differ-di`) implemented
a superset of this ticket and passed LLM-Review +1 / Verified +1; cherry-pick only the scalar
subset from it, dropping the port field helpers.

## Scope

- src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/_backend.py (port: project/query_project/assert_env_ready + BackendAssigneeNotFoundError)
- src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/adapters/jira/backend.py (JiraBackend implements the new members)
- src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/adapters/jira/acli_subprocess.py (AssigneeNotFoundError subclasses the neutral base)
- src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/fetcher.py, applier.py, apply_handlers.py, _attestation.py (rewire; drop vendor imports)
- tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/test_backend_neutrality.py (full-tree AST gate for the four modules)
- tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/ (S2 contract suite + FakeBackend updated to the widened Protocol)
- docs/adr/0035-reconciler-vendor-adapter-seam.md (§(d) interface table: new scalar members + neutral exceptions)

## Testing

- Happy path: `JiraBackend.project` returns the DIG-defaulted project; `query_project` returns
  the configured project; `assert_env_ready()` passes with all three JIRA_* set.
- Failure/edge: `query_project` preserves today's un-defaulted fail-closed behavior when the
  project is unset; `assert_env_ready()` raises naming exactly the missing JIRA_* vars;
  `apply_handlers` catches `BackendAssigneeNotFoundError` when the Jira subclass is raised
  (assignee soft-fail preserved).
- Gate: the new AST neutrality case fails if any of the four modules re-grows an
  `adapters.jira` import (module-level or nested).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `fetcher.py`, `applier.py`, `apply_handlers.py`, `_attestation.py` contain NO
      `adapters.jira`/`acli_subprocess` import at ANY nesting depth — verified by the new
      full-tree `ast.walk` case (not the top-level-only `_module_level_imported_names`
      helper) in `tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/test_backend_neutrality.py`.
- [ ] The Backend port exposes `project`, `query_project`, `assert_env_ready()` (raising
      `BackendEnvError`), and `BackendAssigneeNotFoundError`; `JiraBackend` implements them
      by delegating to `resolve_jira_settings`; the S2 contract suite and `FakeBackend` are
      updated to the widened Protocol; behavior is identical (DIG default in the applier
      guard, fail-closed fetcher JQL scoping, `_attestation` JIRA_* fail-fast, assignee
      soft-fail) — verified by the unit tests named in Testing.
- [ ] ADR 0035 §(d)'s interface table documents the new scalar members and both neutral
      exceptions — verified by the amended ADR text in the change.
- [ ] `make lint && make typecheck && make test` green; whole test tree collects with 0 errors.

## Landed

Implemented and merged to `main` as Gerrit change 926 (LLM-Review +1, Verified +1).
